intended_use: The Philips CT 3500 is a Computed Tomography X-Ray System intended to produce images of the head and body by computer reconstruction of X-Ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. Th
intended_use_full: The Philips CT 3500 is a Computed Tomography X-Ray System intended to produce images of the head and body by computer reconstruction of X-Ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. The Philips CT 3500 is indicated for head, whole body, cardiac (Cardiac Calcium Scoring) and vascular X-ray Computed Tomography applications in patients of all ages. These scanners are intended to be used for diagnostic imaging and for low dose CT lung cancer screening for the early detection of lung nodules that may represent cancer.
target_condition: General diagnostic imaging including head and body imaging, cardiac calcium scoring, vascular imaging, and lung nodule detection for cancer screening
other_metrics: Image noise ≤0.18% at 120kV CTDIcenter (head) ≤33mGy with 10mm image thickness; spatial resolution: high resolution mode 16 lp/cm, standard resolution mode 13 lp/cm; slice thickness range 0.67mm–5mm (helical) and 0.625mm–10.0mm (axial); scan field of view up to 500mm; image matrix up to 1024×1024; minimum scan time 0.5 sec for 360° rotation
